lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Hello, young [sir/lady/one]. Did you want to work on my farm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about your farm.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: I grow fruit here. Special fruit. It grows very fast, but it needs a lot of tending, otherwise it gets the blight and dies.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: These days I'm getting old and slow. You can see all the dead plants from where I've tried to farm them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Life here seems rather peaceful.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: It is most of the time. Something very strange did happen recently though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah? What happened?

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Well, you see... Two strange hooded figures recently visited the farm. They had an odd smell to them and kept their faces hidden.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: They said they represented some client from the far east and that they wanted to inspect the farm. They had a good look around but seemed rather disappointed when they left.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... I'm sure it was nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: I'm a fruit farmer. But now I'm a bit too old for that. Maybe I should try writing books again, like I did when I was younger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to work on your farm.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Well, I'm afraid you're not really suitable.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Alright. Take a handful of seeds from the table, and head into the farm. When you've harvested some fruit, deposit it straight into the sacks.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Remember, you have to water the plants at each stage, otherwise they die of the blight. Fertiliser makes them grow faster... or die faster if you don't water them.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: You'll gain a little extra experience if you grow enough of the fruit successfully.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you any rewards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to go.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: What on earth are you wearing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to do some tithing. Blood tithing.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Well this is not that kind of farm! I only let normal people work here, so get lost!

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Oh, yes, I remember writing that a long time ago. I told the publisher it should be in the public domain, so that everyone could share the knowledge.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Of course, that was before I got so old and found I couldn't afford to hire workers for my farm. Oh well, I hope it helped someone. It wasn't much use to anyone around here.

sections.title: Kharedst's memoirs (with Lunch by the lancalliums included)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you the 'Gric' this entry mentions?

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Yes, I was friends with the last king, it was very sad when he died. The accusations aimed at poor little Rose were ridiculous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Accusations?

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: It was all nonesense, the king died naturally! He was old, he'd been on the throne for at least 40 years.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting, so he must have waited a while before having children?

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Indeed, he married late and his wife... Unfortunately didn't survive bringing Rose into this world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh right, have you seen Rose recently?

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: No... After the incident she was locked away. Most have forgotten about her now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information, Gric. Perhaps it'll all make sense once I've found the other pages.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Perhaps.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Ah, fertiliser. I use a lot of that, but I get mine from Hosidius. If you want to give yours to them, go to their storehouse near the bank.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: I'll give you 60 of my reward points for that.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: I'll give you 120 of my reward points for that.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: I'll give you 120 of my reward points for that.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: I'll give you 100 of my reward points for that.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: I'll give you 40 of my reward points for that.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Take some seeds from the table, otherwise you can't do any farming in there!

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Hey, young [sir/lady/one], do you know what you're doing in there?

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Grow those seeds in the patches. When you've harvested some fruit, deposit it straight into the sacks.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Remember, you have to water the plants at each stage, otherwise they die of the blight. Fertiliser makes them grow faster... or die faster if you don't water them.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: You'll gain a little extra experience if you grow enough of the fruit successfully. So, are you ready to start?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I know what I'm doing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm an expert - don't ask me this again.

lines.speaker: Farmer Gricoller
lines.text: Alright, young [sir/lady/one], I won't. Talk to me if you ever want a reminder.
